_comment: Manual legal review of requirements whose DORIS description carries an ambiguous era annotation (e.g. 'Required reports from Jan 2020 to Nov 2020'). The annotation can mean the requirement lapsed at the end of that window, or that DORIS simply stopped receiving a report still owed. The only way to tell is to read the authorizing law. Each entry below records a reading of the CURRENT text of the cited section. Keyed by 'AGENCY||REPORT NAME' exactly as they appear in data.json. build.py attaches these to the matching record as `law_review`; entries that no longer match any requirement are reported at build time so this file cannot rot silently.

_verdicts.in_force: The cited section still mandates the recurring report. The computed overdue status stands, and the era annotation reflects DORIS's filing history rather than a legal endpoint.
_verdicts.lapsed: The section was repealed or the duty expired by its own terms. Overdue would be wrong.
_verdicts.uncertain: The law text does not resolve it. Keep the caveat.

_method: Current section text read on American Legal Publishing (codelibrary.amlegal.com), the official NYC code publisher, supplemented where noted by City Clerk-certified enacted local-law texts from intro.nyc. Note that amlegal returns HTTP 403 to automated fetching; some sections were read via the BetaNYC nyc-charter mirror, whose index lagged (built 2026-05-19) and missed at least one pending-amendment editor's note. Where the mirror was the only source, confidence is noted accordingly.

reviews.key: Civilian Complaint Review Board (CCRB)||Administrative Prosecution Unit Reports
reviews.cite: CCRB-NYPD Administrative Prosecution Unit MOU (agreed 4/2/2012), para. 18
reviews.quote: CCRB shall provide to the NYPD status reports on the prosecution of its cases, quarterly and as otherwise requested by the NYPD.
reviews.note: Not a statute. Paragraph 18 is an open-ended quarterly duty, but para. 29 lets either party terminate on written notice, and a termination would not necessarily be public — hence medium confidence. CCRB still presents the 2012 MOU as operative and is publishing APU quarterly reports through 2026 Q1, so the report exists; it is simply not being filed with DORIS.

reviews.key: Environmental Protection, Department of (DEP)||Maintenance, Costs and Expenses
reviews.quote: The commissioner of environmental protection shall in every calendar month file in the office of the comptroller an account of any expenses made by the commissioner, or under the commissioner's authority, and of all liabilities incurred by the commissioner during the preceding month and an abstract of each such account shall be published in the City Record.
reviews.note: Recurring monthly duty, no sunset. DORIS's label ('Maintenance, Costs and Expenses') does not match the statute's actual heading, 'Report to comptroller of expenses and liabilities'; the filing goes to the comptroller with an abstract in the City Record.

reviews.key: Fire Department, New York City (FDNY)||Report on Response Times to Fire and Medical Emergencies/Citywide Performance Indicators
reviews.quote: The department shall submit a monthly and yearly report to the council and to the mayor that it shall also post on its website, detailing the citywide response times for each category required herein, disaggregated by borough, community district, and division.
reviews.note: The Ariel Russo Emergency 9-1-1 Response Time Reporting Act. Amended by L.L. 2017/179 to expand the reporting categories — strengthening the duty, not ending it.

reviews.key: Homeless Services, Department of (DHS)||Shelter Repair Scorecard/Report of Deficiencies in Any Homeless Shelters for Adults
reviews.quote: The commissioner shall submit to the speaker of the council quarterly reports summarizing the health, sanitation, safety and fire protection-related deficiencies identified in any inspection of a shelter for adults conducted by any state agency... The first such report shall be due thirty business days following the calendar quarter ending September thirtieth, nineteen hundred and ninety-eight and all subsequent reports shall be due thirty business days following the last day of each succeeding calendar quarter.
reviews.note: 'Each succeeding calendar quarter' is explicitly open-ended; the 1998 date is the start of the obligation, not its end.

reviews.key: Police Department (NYPD)||Index crime clearance report
reviews.quote: No later than 30 days after the quarter ending December 31, 2017 and 30 days after every quarter thereafter, the department shall publish on the department's website a report for the prior quarter, which shall include a report for each borough, of the clearance rate for the following crimes:
reviews.note: 'Every quarter thereafter' is open-ended; the only dated language is a start date. No sunset.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Report on Preventive Services Surveys
reviews.quote: No later than six months following the first administration of the survey required pursuant to this section, and annually thereafter, ACS shall submit to the speaker of the council and post on its website aggregated data from such surveys and any steps ACS had taken in response to the information provided in such surveys.
reviews.note: Subsection (d) unrepealed; 'annually thereafter' is open-ended.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Report on Preventive Services
reviews.quote: Beginning with the calendar quarter ending March 31, 2018, and for every calendar quarter thereafter, ACS shall furnish to the speaker of the council and post on ACS' website, no later than 30 days after the end of each such calendar quarter, a report regarding preventive services rendered during such calendar quarter
reviews.note: No sunset. The 'Jul 2018 - Jan 2021' note describes DORIS's receipts, not the duty.

reviews.key: Management and Budget, Office of (OMB)||Expense Budget Administration
reviews.quote: Each agency shall administer all monies appropriated or available for programs and purposes of the agency in accordance with quarterly allotment plans proposed by the agency and approved or modified by the mayor.
reviews.note: Recurring quarterly duty, no sunset. Medium confidence: the section mandates quarterly allotment plans rather than a document it calls a 'report', so the mapping to DORIS's title is an inference.

reviews.key: Taxi and Limousine Commission (TLC)||Report on the Number of Criminal & Civil Actions for Illegally Operating Non-TLC Licensed Vehicles for Hire (Commuter Vans)
reviews.quote: On or before February 1, 2013 and on a quarterly basis thereafter, the commission shall post on its website and provide to the council a report for the prior quarter
reviews.note: Amended L.L. 2017/008 and L.L. 2019/041 — continued legislative maintenance, not lapse.

reviews.key: Human Resources Administration (HRA)||Cash assistance application and caseload engagement status reports for individuals aged 16 to 25
reviews.quote: Beginning no later than April 1, 2013, and no later than the first day of each subsequent month, the department shall post on its website an updated report regarding the engagement status of heads of household between and including the ages of sixteen and twenty-four
reviews.note: No sunset: LL 51/2012 closes with 'This local law shall take effect immediately.' Two corrections to DORIS's own metadata: subdivision (b) is MONTHLY (the semiannual duty is (c)), and the age range is 16 through 24, not 16 to 25.

reviews.key: Criminal Justice, Mayor's Office of (MOCJ)||Report of the Mayor's Office of Special Enforcement on Inspections of Nightlife Establishments
reviews.quote: Within one month after the calendar year quarter ending December 31, 2019, and within one month of each quarter thereafter, the office shall ensure that the office of special enforcement submits to the council and posts to its website, in a machine readable format, a report on inspections of nightlife establishments overseen by the office of special enforcement.
reviews.note: DORIS's citation (§ 9-309) is wrong: that number is an unrelated district attorney reporting section added by L.L. 2021/161. LL 220/2019 as enacted said '§ 9-307', already taken by L.L. 2019/192, so the codifier placed it at § 9-308. Duty amended effective 1/5/2024 — affirmatively alive.

reviews.key: Transportation, Department of (DOT)||Accessible Water Borne Commuter Services Compliance Report
reviews.quote: The department, the commission on human rights and operators of any water borne commuter services facility each shall provide the mayor and the city council with a semiannual report by January thirty-first and July first of each year setting forth information regarding compliance and non-compliance with this chapter at each water borne commuter services facility
reviews.note: Fixed recurring dates each year; no sunset. Read via the BetaNYC mirror (amlegal 403).

reviews.key: Cultural Affairs, Department of (DCLA)||Quarterly Equal Employment Opportunity and Diversity Plan Implementation
reviews.quote: The head of each city agency shall quarterly publish and submit to the mayor, council, department of citywide administrative services, and the equal employment practices commission a report on the agency's efforts during the previous quarter to implement the plan adopted pursuant to paragraph nineteen of subdivision a of section eight hundred fifteen.
reviews.note: Section 815 amended repeatedly and recently (L.L. 2019/012, 2023/027, 2024/075, 2024/076) without touching subdivision (i). Applies to every city agency, so this reading governs all 422 requirements in the list citing Charter 815.

reviews.key: Immigrant Affairs, Mayor's Office of (MOIA)||Quarterly Equal Employment Opportunity and Diversity Plan Implementation
reviews.quote: The head of each city agency shall quarterly publish and submit to the mayor, council, department of citywide administrative services, and the equal employment practices commission a report on the agency's efforts during the previous quarter to implement the plan adopted pursuant to paragraph nineteen of subdivision a of section eight hundred fifteen.
reviews.note: Same provision as the DCLA row above.

reviews.key: Economic Development Corporation (EDC)||Quarterly Expenditure Reports
reviews.quote: Any public or private agency, authority, corporation, board or commission which receives city funds and is not otherwise subject to the requirements of section one hundred six of this chapter shall submit quarterly reports of the expenditure of such funds to the mayor in such form and detail as the mayor may prescribe.
reviews.note: DORIS's citation (Charter § 119) does not exist — Chapter 6 runs § 100-111. The duty described is § 110, which is in force and covers EDC as a private corporation receiving city funds. Medium confidence because the intended provision is inferred from subject matter.

reviews.key: Finance, Department of (DOF)||Report on revocations
reviews.quote: Not less than quarterly, the department of finance shall report to the speaker of the council and to the mayor a plan and a timeline for revocation of benefits under section 421-a of the real property tax law for each designated building for which such department received, during the reporting period, a final notice of revocation of such benefits
reviews.note: DORIS's citation ('Title 11, Chapter 140') is really § 11-140. LL 193/2017's only timing clause is an effective date, not an expiration.

reviews.key: Health and Hospitals Corporation (H+H)||Report on Health Services in Correctional Facilities
reviews.quote: The department shall submit to the mayor and the speaker of the council no later than July 15, 2015, and every three months thereafter, a report regarding the medical and mental health services provided to incarcerated individuals in city correctional facilities during the previous three calendar months
reviews.note: Enactment note confirms LL 58/2015 created § 17-199 itself (not § 17-199.1). Only later change was a 2021 terminology amendment.

reviews.key: Buildings, Department of (DOB)||Report on Activities of the Office of the Tenant Advocate
reviews.quote: The office of the tenant advocate shall submit to the mayor and the speaker of the city council, and publish on the department's website, quarterly reports related to the responsibilities of the office, including but not limited to:
reviews.note: No amendment or repeal since enactment; the reporting subsection has never been narrowed.

reviews.key: Homeless Services, Department of (DHS)||Report on unsheltered homeless population
reviews.quote: No later than September 1, 2018, and quarterly thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council and post online the total number of unsheltered homeless persons included in the record required pursuant to this subdivision, disaggregated to the extent available by the location where outreach staff first engaged the unsheltered homeless person.
reviews.note: Open-ended. An editor's note shows amendment by L.L. 2026/099 effective 5/30/2028 — does not affect the current duty, but re-check before then.

reviews.key: Administrative Trials and Hearings, Office of (OATH)||Report on ECB Adjudications of Summonses Issued to Vendors
reviews.quote: Commencing on June 1, 2013, and every three months thereafter, the environmental control board shall issue quarterly reports to the council with respect to violations that were issued pursuant to this subchapter or to subchapter two of chapter three of title seventeen of the administrative code, and that were adjudicated by the environmental control board.
reviews.note: Duty falls on the Environmental Control Board, which sits within OATH — the tracker's agency attribution is right.

reviews.key: Correction, Department of (DOC)||Visitation Report
reviews.quote: The commissioner shall post on the department website on a quarterly basis, within 30 days of the beginning of each quarter, a report containing information pertaining to the visitation of the incarcerated individual population in city jails for the prior quarter.
reviews.note: Subdivision (b) was re-enacted by L.L. 2025/044 effective 1/6/2026 — the Council affirmatively kept the quarterly duty alive.

reviews.key: Protect Tenants, Mayor's Office to (MOPT)||Three-quarter Housing Task Force Report
reviews.quote: A city agency or office designated by the mayor shall provide to the speaker of the council and publish on its website quarterly reports on the state of three-quarter housing in the city. The first such report shall be due 30 days following the calendar quarter ending September 30, 2020, and all subsequent reports shall be due 30 days following the last day of each succeeding calendar quarter.
reviews.note: LL 189/2019 has no sunset. The statute assigns the duty to 'a city agency or office designated by the mayor', not to MOPT by name, so MOPT's reorganization does not extinguish it — though the agency attribution on this row may be stale.

reviews.key: Human Resources Administration (HRA)||Report on arrests, summonses, removals, escorts and use of force incidents in job centers and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) centers
reviews.quote: No later than January 31, 2020, and no later than 30 days after the end of each calendar quarter thereafter, the department shall post on its website and submit to the speaker of the council an aggregate report on arrests, summonses, removals, escorts and use of force incidents in job centers and SNAP centers for the preceding quarter.
reviews.note: The Jan 2020 date is a start date. Subdivision (c) requires the reports be 'stored permanently', implying an ongoing series.

reviews.key: Taxi and Limousine Commission (TLC)||Report on Review of Vision Zero Crash Data Involving TLC-Licensed Vehicles Where Critical Injury or Death Occurs
reviews.quote: On or before February 1, 2015 and quarterly thereafter, the commission shall provide to the speaker of the council and shall place on the commission's website, a written report detailing how many crashes the commission reviewed pursuant to subdivision a of this section in the prior quarter, how many summary suspensions occurred, and enforcement actions taken by the commission following such review.
reviews.note: Codified text is verbatim identical to the 2014 enactment — the reporting subsection has never been amended away.

reviews.key: Health and Mental Hygiene, Department of (DOHMH)||Community Quality Air Survey
reviews.quote: The department shall conduct a community air quality survey on an annual basis. ... Beginning April 22, 2016, and on or before April 22 annually thereafter, the department shall submit to the speaker of the council a report with the results of the annual community air quality survey for the most recently available year's analysis.
reviews.note: Original enactment credit, no amendments or repeal. Expressly recurring.

reviews.key: Consumer and Worker Protection, Department of (DCWP)||Annual Report on Paid Care Industry
reviews.quote: The division shall post on the division's website and submit to the speaker of the council, beginning no later than one year after the effective date of this law and annually thereafter, the following information for the preceding calendar year:
reviews.note: No sunset. The surrounding chapter is live — § 32-202 amended as recently as L.L. 2020/080, so the Division of Paid Care remains an operating statutory body.

reviews.key: Taxi and Limousine Commission (TLC)||Crash Data Involving TLC-Licensed Vehicles
reviews.quote: On or before February 1, 2015 and quarterly thereafter, the commission shall for the prior quarter provide a report to the council and shall post on its website, the number of crashes involving a vehicle licensed by the commission.
reviews.note: No expiration. The parallel quarterly mandate in § 19-540(b) survives as well.

reviews.key: Human Resources Administration (HRA)||Emergency Feeding Programs Application Distribution Report
reviews.quote: Beginning January 1, 2006, and on the first business day of each succeeding calendar quarter thereafter, the commissioner shall submit a report to the speaker of the city council indicating the emergency feeding programs to which it distributed applications in the prior calendar quarter and the number of applications distributed to each emergency feeding program.
reviews.note: Open-ended, no sunset. Note there are two sections designated § 21-131; the food stamp one is the correct match and is intact.

reviews.key: Police Department (NYPD)||Report on Random Audits of Special Victims Division Case Management System
reviews.quote: The department shall conduct quarterly random audits of the case management system to ensure the security of such system. Such audit shall include a review of all individuals outside of the special victims division who have accessed special victims division case files during the previous quarter. The department shall report the results of the audit to the council within 60 days of the end of each quarter.
reviews.note: DORIS's citation (§ 14-177(c)) is wrong: § 14-177 is 'Harassment and sexual assault survivor sensitivity training' (L.L. 2018/189) and has no subsection (c) and no reporting duty. LL 192/2018 added § 14-178. No sunset — the enacted law's only closing provision is an effective date.

reviews.key: Economic Development Corporation (EDC)||Report on Lobbying Activities by the Economic Development Corporation
reviews.quote: The first such submission shall be provided no later than December 31, 2013 and shall cover the period from November 1, 2012 through November 30, 2013. All reports shall be filed thereafter within thirty days after the end of each quarter and simultaneously made available in a commonly available non-proprietary database format on the website of the contracted entity
reviews.note: LL 222/2017 did not curtail the duty — it merely redesignated §§ 22-801 and 22-802 into a new subchapter 1; its repeals target Charter § 1301(1)(b) and (b-1). No expiration.

reviews.key: Environmental Protection, Department of (DEP)||Quarterly Report on Combined Sewer Outfalls
reviews.quote: The department shall, within eight months of the effective date of this section, complete an analysis of the combined sewer outfall system and its relationship to floatables, including medical debris ... This analysis shall include, but not be limited to, identifying, evaluating and providing: ... 6. A quarterly report about the status of this debris, as evidenced by the city-wide floatables study
reviews.note: Not repealed and no sunset — and the code flags repeals explicitly (adjacent § 24-504.1 is marked '[Repealed]'), so it did not silently lapse. But the quarterly report is item 6 in an enumerated list of what a ONE-TIME analysis 'shall include', not a freestanding perpetual duty. The text cannot resolve whether the quarterly cadence survives completion of that study. Two independent reviewers reached this same conclusion.

reviews.key: Environmental Protection, Department of (DEP)||Combined sewer overflow and floatables, including medical debris, study
reviews.quote: The department shall, within eight months of the effective date of this section, complete an analysis of the combined sewer outfall system and its relationship to floatables, including medical debris ... This analysis shall include, but not be limited to, identifying, evaluating and providing: ... 6. A quarterly report about the status of this debris, as evidenced by the city-wide floatables study
reviews.note: Same provision as the row above. Additionally, DORIS attributes this row to the 'CSO Order on Consent' — a state DEC consent order, not a local-law reporting mandate. Any ongoing quarterly CSO obligation would arise from that order, which is outside what the code text can resolve.

reviews.key: Police Department (NYPD)||Use of Force Encounter Report
reviews.quote: Use of force encounter report. The department shall post on its website within 30 days of the beginning of each quarter a report of the number of use of force incidents for the prior quarter disaggregated by basis for encounter.
reviews.note: Open-ended ('each quarter'); the only editor's note points to unconsolidated provisions, not a repeal.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Quarterly Detention Incident Reports/Close-To-Home Incident Report
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 21-906(a) (rewritten by L.L. 2024/112, eff. 1/1/2025)
reviews.quote: By January 15, 2025, and no later than 60 days after the end of each quarter thereafter, ACS shall submit a report related to incidents occurring in juvenile justice facilities to the speaker of the council and make such report available on ACS's website.
reviews.note: DORIS's law fields (LL 14/2010, LL 44/2013) are STALE. Far from lapsing, the Council refreshed and expanded this mandate in Nov 2024 with a new Jan 15, 2025 baseline.

reviews.key: Housing Preservation and Development, Department of (HPD)||List of properties with 20 or more units identified by block and lot number with identity of mortgagee plaintiffs for which notice has been provided under 27-2109.1(a)
reviews.quote: The department shall maintain on its website a list of all properties with twenty or more units, identified by block and lot number ... Such list shall be updated at a minimum on the first business day of each month.
reviews.note: LL 4/2012's final section is an effective-date clause only — no sunset. Verified against the City Clerk-certified enacted text.

reviews.key: Correction, Department of (DOC)||Report of the Board of Correction
reviews.quote: The board, annually and at such other times as it may determine, shall submit to the mayor, the council, and the commissioner reports, findings and recommendations in regard to the matters within its jurisdiction.
reviews.note: DORIS supplies no citation and its 'LL 102/1977' attribution is not the operative source; the duty is Charter-based and was amended as recently as Nov 2025.

reviews.key: Small Business Services, Department of (SBS)||Annual Report on the Locally Based Enterprises Program
reviews.quote: The mayor shall submit an annual report to the council, on or before April first of each year, concerning the administration of the program established pursuant to this section.
reviews.note: Intact. The adjacent § 6-108.2 carries an explicit '[Repealed]' tag from L.L. 2023/067, showing the code marks repeals clearly and left this one alone.

reviews.key: Small Business Services, Department of (SBS)||Report on Activities of the Division of Economic and Financial Opportunity
reviews.cite: NYC Charter § 1304(e)(5) — REPEALED by L.L. 2023/069, eff. 6/28/2023
reviews.note: Repealed as one of the reports selected for waiver by the Report and Advisory Board Review Commission. The paragraph now reads only 'reserved'. DORIS's listed authorities (LL 12/2006, LL 129/2005, LL 61/1991) are superseded on this point.

reviews.key: Contract Services, Mayor's Office of (MOCS)||Anticipated Contracting Actions for the Upcoming Fiscal Year
reviews.quote: The mayor shall, no later than July 31st of each year, produce and publish on the mayor's office of contract services website a plan and schedule for each agency detailing the anticipated contracting actions of each such agency for the upcoming fiscal year.
reviews.note: Affirmatively expanded by LL 2024/085 — decisive evidence it is live.

reviews.key: Human Resources Administration (HRA)||Cash assistance application and caseload engagement status reports for individuals aged 16 to 20
reviews.quote: Cash assistance caseload engagement status report for heads of household aged sixteen through twenty-four. Beginning no later than April 1, 2013, and no later than the first day of each subsequent month, the department shall post on its website an updated report regarding the engagement status of heads of household between and including the ages of sixteen and twenty-four
reviews.note: DORIS's title is wrong: subdivision (b) covers ages 16 through 24, not 16 to 20. The '16 to 20' range belongs to subdivision (c), a separate and also in-force semiannual duty. LL 51/2012 has no sunset.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Annual Detention Incident Reports/Close-to-Home Annual
reviews.quote: Annual incident reports. 1. Within 60 days after the end of each fiscal year, ACS shall post a report on its website containing the following data: (a) the number of allegations made during the fiscal year that a child in a detention or placement facility was a neglected or abused child;
reviews.note: DORIS's '(a) and (b)' is imprecise for an annual report: (a) is the quarterly duty, (b) the annual, (c) a monthly OC-spray report. Expanded, not narrowed, by LL 2024/112.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Demographic Data on Population Placements and Transfers
reviews.quote: By January 15, 2025, and no later than 90 days after the end of each quarter thereafter, ACS shall submit a report about the demographics of juveniles in the juvenile justice system to the speaker of the council and post such report on ACS's website.
reviews.note: Two DORIS errors: the section has lettered subdivisions a-g, not paragraphs (1)/(2); and the FREQUENCY is wrong — this is quarterly, not annual, since LL 2024/112 re-based it to Jan 2025. The annual compilation duty sits in § 21-903.

reviews.key: Police Department (NYPD)||Patrol Guide Procedures Quarterly Report
reviews.cite: Former NYC Admin. Code § 14-150(a)(2) — REPEALED by L.L. 2016/129, eff. 1/29/2017
reviews.quote: Section 1. Paragraph 2 of subdivision a of section 14-150 of the administrative code of the city of New York is REPEALED.
reviews.note: Verified independently against the current § 14-150: the words 'patrol guide' appear nowhere in the section, (a)(2) is now the crime status report, and the credit line includes 'Am. L.L. 2016/129, eff. 1/29/2017'. The surviving quarterly duty at (f)(1) covers training materials only. The replacement, § 14-164, is a 24-hour website-update duty with no reporting cadence. The 2021 filing postdates the repeal. DORIS's law fields never record the 2016 repealer.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Annual Reports Regarding Youth Aging Out of Foster Care
reviews.quote: Annual reports regarding youth and foster care. Beginning no later than April 30, 2026 for the calendar year 2025 and annually thereafter, ACS shall furnish to the speaker of the council and the public advocate, and post on ACS's website a report regarding youth in foster care.
reviews.note: Amended Aug 2025, re-basing the due date to April 30, 2026 and adding data points. Note the BetaNYC mirror conflates § 21-902.1 with § 21-902; amlegal is reliable here.

reviews.key: End Domestic and Gender-Based Violence, Mayor's Office to (ENDGBV)||Domestic Violence Fatality Review Committee Report on Domestic Violence Initiatives, Indicators and Factors
reviews.quote: On June 1, 2019, and no later than January 31 annually thereafter, the office shall submit to the mayor and speaker of the council and post on its website an annual report regarding certain domestic violence initiatives, indicators, and factors.
reviews.note: DORIS's citation (§ 3-171(b)(1)) is WRONG — § 3-171 is 'Pay and employment equity data' and has nothing to do with domestic violence. The provision enacted by LL 38/2019 is § 3-181.

reviews.key: Records and Information Services, Department of (DORIS)||Bulletin on activities (Library Notes)
reviews.quote: provide for the distribution of publications of the city, where such authority is not vested in another city agency, and issue at regular intervals, no less than quarterly, a bulletin describing its facilities and resources;
reviews.note: Standing duty at a fixed minimum cadence. Section amended Aug 2025 — demonstrably live.

reviews.key: Environmental Protection, Department of (DEP)||Commissioner report on all waivers, findings, and renewals relating to clean heating oil and biodiesel
reviews.quote: No later than September 1, 2013, and no later than September 1 of every year thereafter, the commissioner shall submit a report to the mayor and the speaker of the council, which shall include: (i) all waivers, findings and renewals of such findings issued pursuant to this section during the immediately preceding calendar year;
reviews.note: Neighboring §§ 24-170 to 24-172 carry '[Repealed]' tags from L.L. 2015/038; this section is intact and was amended in 2023. The duty may be satisfied via the Mayor's Management Report under (d)(2), a plausible explanation for the sparse DORIS record.

reviews.key: Children's Services, Administration for (ACS)||Accountability Review Panel/Annual Child Fatality Review Report
reviews.quote: No later than June 1, 2018, and no later than 18 months after the end of each calendar year thereafter, ACS shall publish and make available on its website a report on the findings and recommendations of its child fatality reviews during the previous calendar year.
reviews.note: ACS has in fact continued publishing these — its 2022 and 2023 Systemic Child Fatality Review reports both cite § 21-915 — so the report exists but is not reaching DORIS. The title's 'Accountability Review Panel' element is a DORIS conflation; § 21-915 governs only the child fatality review report.

reviews.key: Correction, Department of (DOC)||Post-Arraignment Bail Holds
reviews.quote: Beginning July 1, 2018, the department or its designee shall submit to the council an annual report regarding the implementation of subdivisions a and b of this section.
reviews.note: DORIS's attribution to 'LL 85 of 2015 (Int. 706)' is WRONG — that law is codified at § 9-140 and covers jail visitation, not bail. The duty comes from LL 124/2017. Medium confidence: LL 124/2017 carries a CONDITIONAL sunset that only triggers once the correction commissioner notifies the Council; no such notice was found, and amlegal still prints subd. (d) unmarked, with a state amendment dated 8/8/2022 postdating the trigger date.

reviews.key: Immigrant Affairs, Mayor's Office of (MOIA)||Report on Agency Policies on Identifying Information
reviews.quote: No later than July 31, 2018, and every two years thereafter by July 31, each city agency shall provide a report regarding the collection, retention, and disclosure of identifying information by such agency and any contractors or subcontractors utilized by such agency.
reviews.note: No sunset. Companion § 23-1204(c) reinforces the ongoing cycle. Attribution caveat: the duty runs to 'each city agency', not MOIA specifically.

reviews.key: Operations, Mayor's Office of (OPS)||Report of the Algorithms Management and Policy Officer
reviews.cite: Executive Order 50 of 2019 § 4 — REVOKED by Executive Order 3 of 2022 § 10 (Jan. 19, 2022)
reviews.quote: Executive Order No. 50, dated November 19, 2019, are hereby REVOKED. § 11. This Order shall take effect immediately.
reviews.note: Rests on a mayoral executive order, not a statute. EO 3 of 2022 both discontinued the office (§ 4: 'The Algorithms Management and Policy Officer ... shall be discontinued') and revoked EO 50 outright (§ 10). No local law preserves the report. The residual function went to OTI, so DORIS's attribution to the Mayor's Office of Operations is wrong even as to the successor. Same executive order that dissolved DOITT, MODA and MOIP.

reviews.key: Food Policy Director, Mayor's Office of the (MOFP)||City Food Metrics Report (Not the same as the food security portion attributed to Office of Food Policy?)
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 3-120(a) (L.L. 2011/052; Am. L.L. 2021/059) and NYC Charter § 20(j)
reviews.quote: No later than September first, two thousand twelve, and no later than every September first thereafter, the office of long-term planning and sustainability shall prepare and submit to the mayor and the speaker of the city council a report regarding the production, processing, distribution and consumption of food in and for the city of New York during the previous fiscal year.
reviews.note: LL 2021/059 EXPANDED the report (adding SNAP gap, food-insecurity and dietary-outcome measures) rather than winding it down. Attribution caveat: § 3-120 places the duty on the office of long-term planning and sustainability, though MOFP publishes it in practice.

reviews.key: Records and Information Services, Department of (DORIS)||Municipal Archives Annual Report (contained within the Archival Review Board annual report)
reviews.quote: Such board shall render annually to the mayor a report reviewing the archival processing of any city papers during the year for which the report has been written.
reviews.note: L.L. 2026/077 (eff. 8/7/2026) renames the body and RE-ENACTS the annual report as a new subdivision e ('No later than December 31, 2026, and annually by December 31 thereafter'). The report survives the reorganization — the law repeals § 3009, not this duty.

reviews.key: Fire Department, New York City (FDNY)||Report on Recruitment Efforts
reviews.quote: The following information shall be provided on March 1 of each year for the preceding calendar year: 1. The department's expenditures on recruiting candidates for the open-competitive firefighter civil service examination and promotion to firefighter civil service examination;
reviews.note: DORIS's 'Section 130(3)' is a mangle — there is no subdivision 3; the recruitment content is subdivision (c), paragraphs 1-3. LL 2015/049's final section is an effective-date clause only.

reviews.key: Police Department (NYPD)||Firearms Discharge Report
reviews.quote: The New York city police department shall submit to the city council and post on its website on an annual basis a firearms discharge report, which shall include, but need not be limited to, the following information for the prior year:
reviews.note: Subdivision (b) intact and recurring; section maintained through 2024. (Contrast subdivision (a)(2) in the same section, whose patrol guide duty WAS repealed in 2016.)

reviews.key: Fire Department, New York City (FDNY)||Report on materials developed with Department of Education on fire safety and prevention
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 15-133(b) — DEEMED REPEALED June 12, 2023 by L.L. 2018/116 § 2
reviews.quote: Subdivision b of section 15-133 of the administrative code of the city of New York, as added by section one of this local law, is deemed repealed 5 years after it becomes law.
reviews.note: THE SUBTLE ONE. amlegal's running text still prints subdivision (b) as if live ('Beginning January 31, 2019 and annually thereafter') because the self-repealer lives in the law's unconsolidated provisions, not the code page — only the editor's note pointing to Appendix A hints at it. The clerk certifies the law was 'returned unsigned by the Mayor on June 12, 2018', so (b) died June 12, 2023. DORIS's era note ('Reports from Nov 2020 to Jan 2023') is accurate: the Jan 2023 filing was the last one ever owed. Subdivision (a), the duty to run the education program, survives.

reviews.key: City Planning, Department of (DCP)||Zoning and Planning Report
reviews.quote: Not later than the thirty-first day of December, nineteen hundred ninety-two and every four years thereafter, the commission shall file with the mayor, the council, the public advocate, the borough presidents, and community boards, a zoning and planning report.
reviews.note: Four-year cycle confirmed against the text, running from Dec 31 1992 — so due Dec 31 of 2020, 2024, 2028. L.L. 2023/069 does not touch § 192.

reviews.key: Contract Services, Mayor's Office of (MOCS)||Small Purchases Quarterly Report
reviews.cite: NYC Charter § 314(b) — REPEALED by L.L. 2023/069, eff. 6/28/2023
reviews.quote: Section 1. Subdivision b of section 314 of the New York city charter is REPEALED.
reviews.note: The law's title names the target: 'to repeal subdivision b of section 314 of such charter, relating to a report on small purchases'. Charter § 314 today consists of subdivision (a) only and contains no reporting requirement. Another Report and Advisory Board Review Commission waiver.

reviews.key: Homeless Services, Department of (DHS)||Homeless Diversion Teams
reviews.quote: Beginning on October 1, 1995 and on the first day of each succeeding calendar quarter thereafter, the commissioner shall report to the speaker of the city council in writing on the homeless diversion teams including, but not limited to, the following information aggregated on a quarterly and fiscal year annualized basis;
reviews.note: No amendment note at all — the text stands as originally enacted. L.L. 2023/069 does not touch it. Attribution caveat: the duty falls on the Social Services commissioner and the screening happens at HRA centers, so this is properly a DSS/HRA obligation.

reviews.key: Citywide Administrative Services, Department of (DCAS)||Report on NYC Facilities Energy System Assessment of Certain Clean On-site Power Generation Technologies
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 4-207 — REPEALED by L.L. 2023/069, eff. 6/28/2023
reviews.quote: § 4-207 Assessment of certain clean on-site power generation technologies. [Repealed] (Am. L.L. 2022/060, 5/15/2022, eff. 5/15/2022; Repealed L.L. 2023/069, 5/29/2023, eff. 6/28/2023)
reviews.note: Was the single most overdue entry in the tracker at 3,118 days — the first row a reader saw. The section is marked [Repealed] outright. Found not by the era annotation (this row has none) but by sweeping every provision L.L. 2023/069 repealed against the requirements list.

reviews.key: Transportation, Department of (DOT)||Traffic Calming Study
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 19-179 — REPEALED by L.L. 2023/069, eff. 6/28/2023
reviews.quote: § 19-179 Traffic calming study. [Repealed] (Repealed L.L. 2023/069, 5/29/2023, eff. 6/28/2023)
reviews.note: Carried a red 'never filed' badge for a requirement that no longer exists.

reviews.key: Health and Mental Hygiene, Department of (DOHMH)||Electronic Death Registration System Evaluation
reviews.cite: NYC Admin. Code § 17-196(i) — REPEALED by L.L. 2023/069, eff. 6/28/2023
reviews.note: Subdivision (i) now reads only 'Reserved'. The rest of § 17-196 (the electronic death registration system itself) survives; only the reporting subsection died. Carried a red 'never filed' badge.
